verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type I TM protein with its N-terminal EF-hand/SAM domain in the ER lumen and C-terminus in the cytoplasm — dominant localization is the ER membrane. However, STIM1 has a well-documented minority plasma membrane pool (PM-STIM1) detected by surface biotinylation and antibody staining with its EF-hand/SAM domain exposed extracellularly, arising from secretory trafficking. This gives a bona fide outer-leaflet-exposed fraction via its own TM anchor, qualifying as dual_localization. Not 'yes' because ER is the canonical compartment; the surface pool is minor.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Magnitude and therapeutic tractability of the PM-STIM1 pool relative to dominant ER localization is debated.
